---
title: ""
---

# Md Khalid Bin Kamal

PhD Student — Public Policy & Political Economy\
University of Texas at Dallas

[CV](CV.pdf) \| [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=EOaFnycAAAAJ&hl=en) \| [khalid\@utdallas.edu](mailto:khalid@utdallas.edu){.email}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

I study how institutions, policies, and political shocks shape economic outcomes, particularly in developing contexts. My research applies causal inference methods to evaluate real-world policy interventions in governance, labor markets, and welfare systems.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Updates

-   Article on secession and food prices published in *Agricultural and Food Economics* (2025)\
-   Paper on Yemen food price inflation accepted in *Humanities and Social Sciences Communications* (2025)
-   Presenting research at the 2026 Midwest Political Science Association (MPSA) Conference, Chicago.
-   Presenting research at the 2026 Western Social Science Association (WSSA) Conference.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Current Coursework — Spring 2026

-   Knowledge Mining\
-   Economics for Public Policy\
-   Political and Civic Organizations

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Contact

Email: khalid\@utdallas.edu\
Alternate: khalidbinkamal\@gmail.com\
University of Texas at Dallas
